|timeline=[[3 ABY/Legends|3 ABY]]<ref name="Post-ESB">With the exception of certain flashback issues, [[Star Wars (1977)|''Star Wars'' (1977)]] tells a continuous storyline, with issues 39-44 adapting the events of {{Film|V}}, and ''[[The New Essential Chronology]]'' dates both ''The Empire Strikes Back'' and the events of issues 52, 61 and 69 to [[3 ABY/Legends|3 ABY]], thus placing all of issues 39-69 in 3 ABY as well. Additionally, ''[[The New Essential Guide to Characters]]'' places the ''[[Star Wars: Shadows of the Empire]]'' storyline immediately before [[Star Wars (1977) 72|issue 72]], and ''The New Essential Chronology'' dates ''Shadows of the Empire'' to 3.5 ABY.</ref>
|series=[[Star Wars (1977)|''Star Wars'' (1977)]]
|issue=55

{{Quote|Very well, fellows, let's show that grotto-grabbing villain what it means to incur a Hoojib's wrath!|[[Plif/Legends|Plif]]|<ref name="Star Wars (1977) 55">[[Star Wars (1977) 55|''Star Wars'' (1977) 55]]</ref>}}
'''''Star Wars 55: Plif!''''' is the fifty-fifth issue in the [[Star Wars (1977)|''Marvel Star Wars'']] series of comics.

==Plot summary==
[[Princess/Legends|Princess]] [[Leia Organa Solo/Legends|Leia Organa]] leads a survey team to the forest [[Planet/Legends|planet]] known as [[Arbra/Legends|Arbra]]. It is her hope to establish a permanent [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel Alliance]] base of operations after having been forced to evacuate their previous outpost on [[Hoth/Legends|Hoth]]. Leia's crew discover that the woods are inhabited by small, telepathic, rodent-like creatures known as [[Hoojib/Legends|Hoojibs]]. The Hoojibs feed off electricity, so during the evening, they scamper around the survey camp, draining all of the power away from the Rebels' equipment and weaponry. The following morning, Princess Leia encounters a Hoojib named [[Plif/Legends|Plif]]—the [[spokesmind]] for the entire Hoojib population. Plif explains that the Hoojibs thrive off electrical radiation and that they typically feed from a trove of geothermal crystals located in a nearby cave. However, they had recently been forced to abandon the cave due to the presence of a large, flying creature known as a [[Slivilith]]. Leia agrees to help Plif oust the Slivilith so that the Hoojibs can regain their food source and stop draining resources from the rebels.

The Slivilith flies downward into the survey camp and begins attacking the rebels. The officers are practically powerless due to the fact that their [[Blaster/Legends|blasters]] no longer hold a charge. They herd the creature back towards its nesting grotto and keep it at bay by way of non-energy based melee weapons such as lances and pole arms. [[Chewbacca/Legends|Chewbacca]] the [[Wookiee/Legends|Wookiee]] manages to grab the Slivilith by its tentacles and smash it down upon the power rod crystals, killing it. Grateful for their assistance, Plif volunteers to share the environs of Arbra with the Rebel Alliance. Leia reports back to the fleet that they have found a new permanent base of operations.

Meanwhile, [[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker]] has a conversation with [[Lando Calrissian/Legends|Lando Calrissian]]. Lando has had concerns about the state of affairs in [[Cloud City/Legends|Cloud City]] on the planet [[Bespin/Legends|Bespin]] ever since he voluntarily abdicated his position as Baron Administrator. Taking the [[Millennium Falcon/Legends|''Millennium Falcon'']], Lando excuses himself from the fleet and returns to Cloud City.

==Continuity==
This comic book issue includes advertisements for the following products: ''Grit'' Magazine, ''Comic Art Workshop'', ''Creation'' comic convention, Marvel Comics subscription service, ''Bubble Yum'' bubble gum sweepstakes, ''Hostess'' Twinkies Cakes, and Marvel Comics' ''Uncanny X-Men'' #153: ''Kitty's Fairy Tale''.

This issue was later adapted into a book-and-record titled ''[[The Further Adventures: Planet of the Hoojibs]]'', released by [[Buena Vista Records]].
